title: TCP client connect and data transfer
purpose: This brief adds TCP client-side connection and bidirectional data transfer. Connect creates an outbound TCP connection as an FdResource. Send and recv submit Write and Read operations to the CompletionRing for non-blocking, completion-based data transfer. This is the core networking primitive — HTTP clients, database drivers, and OTP's gen_tcp all build on these operations.
task: Implement tcp_connect, tcp_send, and tcp_recv BIFs. Connect submits a Connect operation and returns an FdResource. Send and recv submit Write/Read operations and suspend the process. Handle partial sends, connection resets, and recv timeouts.

requirements.title: tcp_connect BIF
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:tcp_connect/3 is called with host, port, and options, THE SYSTEM SHALL create a socket, submit a Connect operation to the ring, suspend the process, and on completion return {ok, ConnFd} or {error, Reason}.
acceptance: erlang:tcp_connect/3 registered as BIF with args (Host :: binary(), Port :: integer(), Options :: list())
Host is resolved to IP address (DNS resolution on dirty thread for initial implementation)
Socket created with SOCK_STREAM, set non-blocking
Connect operation submitted to ring
Process suspends until connect completes or times out
Returns {ok, FdResource} on success, {error, Reason} on failure
Options: {timeout, Ms}, {ip, LocalAddr}, {port, LocalPort}
Test: connect to a local listener, verify connection established

requirements.title: tcp_send BIF
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:tcp_send/2 is called with a connected FdResource and data binary, THE SYSTEM SHALL submit a Write operation and return ok or {error, Reason}.
acceptance: erlang:tcp_send/2 registered as BIF
Extracts bytes from binary term
Submits Write to the ring with the socket FD
Process suspends until write completes
Returns ok on full send, {error, Reason} on failure
Connection reset (ECONNRESET, EPIPE) returns {error, closed}
Test: connect, send data, verify received on listener side

requirements.title: tcp_recv BIF
requirements.spec: WHEN erlang:tcp_recv/2 or erlang:tcp_recv/3 is called with a connected FdResource and length, THE SYSTEM SHALL submit a Read operation and return {ok, Data} or {error, Reason}.
acceptance: erlang:tcp_recv/2 registered as BIF with args (ConnFd, Length)
erlang:tcp_recv/3 with timeout as third argument
Length = 0 means read whatever is available (non-blocking read size)
Length > 0 means read exactly that many bytes (may require multiple ring submissions)
Returns {ok, Binary} with received data
Returns {error, closed} on connection close (0 bytes read)
Returns {error, timeout} if timeout expires before data arrives
Test: send data from one end, recv on the other, verify match

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement active mode (automatic message delivery) — that is B-108
SHALL NOT implement TCP options (nodelay, keepalive) — that is B-110
SHALL NOT implement scatter-gather send (writev) — future optimization
SHALL NOT implement SSL/TLS — separate concern
verification: cargo test passes with full connect-send-recv cycle between two processes
Send on closed connection returns {error, closed}
Large data transfer (>64KB) completes without corruption
